OFF CALENDAR. THE STODDDARD DECLARATION FAILS TO SHOW THAT THE PARTIES MET AND CONFERRED "IN PERSON, BY TELEPHONE, OR BY VIDEO CONFERENCE" IN COMPLIANCE WITH CCP 430.41. MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Jun-27-2025 Continued To May-26-26 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION FOR LEAVE TO File A Cross-Complaint
Off Calendar Apr-07-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT LONNIE GEORGE, AX9 SECURITY, INC.'s MOTION FOR LEAVE TO File A Cross-Complaint.

Defendant Lonnie George's unopposed Motion for Leave to File Cross-Complaint is GRANTED.

Leave to file a cross-complaint "may be granted in the interest of justice at any time during the course of the action." (CCP Section 428.50(c).) "A motion to file a cross-complaint at any time during the course of the action must be granted unless bad faith of the moving party is demonstrated where forfeiture would otherwise result. Factors such as oversight, inadvertence, neglect, mistake or other cause, are insufficient grounds to deny the motion unless accompanied by bad faith." (Silver Organizations Ltd. v. Frank (1990) 217 Cal.App.3d 94, 99.)

Good cause appearing, Defendant is granted leave to file the proposed cross-complaint. Defendant must file his cross-complaint, substantially identical to Exhibit A to the Choo Declaration, within ten days of entry of this order.

Order is signed.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT CHINESE HOSPITAL ASSOCIATION DBA THE CHINESE HOSPITAL's DEMURRER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Off calendar. The Stodddard declaration fails to show that the parties met and conferred "in person, by telephone, or by video conference" in compliance with CCP 430.41. The parties are ordered to comply with the code. The response to the amended complaint is now due by November 6, 2024.
